[{"review_id": "reviewid_135", "user_id": "userid_548", "business_ref": "businessref_34", "rating": "2", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this \"burger\" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our \"server\" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!", "date": "29 May 2013, 23:01"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_314", "user_id": "userid_1903", "business_ref": "businessref_66", "rating": "2", "useful": "1", "funny": "2", "cool": "1", "text": "This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare", "date": "21 May 2016, 18:48"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_487", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_95", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened", "date": "November 01, 2021 at 05:11 PM"}]

import json
import re

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']

# Filter for businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for business in businesses:
    if business.get('attributes') != 'None' and business.get('attributes') is not None:
        attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
        if isinstance(attributes, dict):
            if attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
                credit_card_businesses.append(business)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({"count": len(credit_card_businesses), "sample": credit_card_businesses[:2]}))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']

# Filter for businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for business in businesses:
    if business.get('attributes') != 'None' and business.get('attributes') is not None:
        attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
        if isinstance(attributes, dict):
            if attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
                credit_card_businesses.append(business)

# Extract categories from descriptions
categories = []
for business in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Find the category list (usually after "services in" or "including")
    match = re.search(r'(services in|including|offers.*services including) (.+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(2)
        # Remove location and other text
        cat_text = cat_text.split('.')[0]  # Remove period and after
        cat_text = cat_text.split(' and ')[-1] if ' and ' in cat_text else cat_text
        # Extract categories
        business_cats = []
        if ',' in cat_text:
            parts = cat_text.split(',')
            for part in parts:
                part = part.strip()
                if part and part != 'and' and not part.startswith('Located at'):
                    # Remove "and" from the beginning
                    if part.startswith('and '):
                        part = part[4:]
                    business_cats.append(part)
        else:
            business_cats = [cat_text.strip()]
        
        # Clean up categories
        cleaned_cats = []
        for cat in business_cats:
            # Remove extra words
            if cat and cat != 'and':
                cleaned_cats.append(cat)
        
        business['categories'] = cleaned_cats
        categories.extend(cleaned_cats)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(categories))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get all businesses that accept credit cards
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']

# Filter for businesses that accept credit cards
credit_card_businesses = []
for business in businesses:
    if business.get('attributes') != 'None' and business.get('attributes') is not None:
        attributes = business.get('attributes', {})
        if isinstance(attributes, dict):
            if attributes.get('BusinessAcceptsCreditCards') == 'True':
                credit_card_businesses.append(business)

# Extract categories from descriptions
categories = []
for business in credit_card_businesses:
    desc = business.get('description', '')
    # Find the category list (usually after "services in" or "including")
    match = re.search(r'(services in|including|offers.*services including) (.+)', desc, re.IGNORECASE)
    if match:
        cat_text = match.group(2)
        # Remove location and other text
        cat_text = cat_text.split('.')[0]  # Remove period and after
        cat_text = cat_text.split(' and ')[-1] if ' and ' in cat_text else cat_text
        # Extract categories
        business_cats = []
        if ',' in cat_text:
            parts = cat_text.split(',')
            for part in parts:
                part = part.strip()
                if part and part != 'and' and not part.startswith('Located at'):
                    # Remove "and" from the beginning
                    if part.startswith('and '):
                        part = part[4:]
                    business_cats.append(part)
        else:
            business_cats = [cat_text.strip()]
        
        # Clean up categories
        cleaned_cats = []
        for cat in business_cats:
            # Remove extra words
            if cat and cat != 'and':
                cleaned_cats.append(cat)
        
        business['categories'] = cleaned_cats
        categories.extend(cleaned_cats)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(categories))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

I've purchased about 10 used cars in the last 25 years. This has been the worst experience by far. They needed to remove pin stripes and fix a tire pressure sensor so they agreed to deliver it; we live almost two hours away. Met them after dark near the highway on 4/2; the check engine light came on on our way home. Texted a pic to the salesman and called him the next day -- no response. Left several voice mails for the warranty/finance guy - no response. Took it to a friend at local dealer who hooked it up to the diagnostic machine and said it was the after-market high-flow air intake that caused the warning light. I would not have purchased the car had I known that. Then another warning light - reduced engine performance, reduce clutch use. To make a very long story short - clutch goes, daughter gets stranded on busy highway, vehicle towed back to Ford of Port Richey; lots of promises about fixing it and being at the front of the service line, zero follow up, 53 days of ownership, 31 days out of commission/in shop and the story ends with a bill for $2000, even though they ultimately acknowledged one of their managers drove the car for three days because the check engine light kept coming on before they sold it. I won't even mention the cracked windshield we saw the morning after they delivered - just turn it in on your insurance. I understand it is free but still counts as a claim. var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re

# Get businesses and reviews
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']

# Read review data if it's a file
if isinstance(reviews, str) and reviews.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(f"Total reviews: {len(reviews)}")
print(f"Sample review keys: {list(reviews[0].keys()) if reviews else 'No reviews'}")

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Get businesses and reviews
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:22']
reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']

# Read review data if it's a file
if isinstance(reviews, str) and reviews.endswith('.json'):
    with open(reviews, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(f"Total reviews: {len(reviews)}")
print(f"Sample review keys: {list(reviews[0].keys()) if reviews else 'No reviews'}")

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

args.code:
import json
import re
import os

# Get the full review data
review_file = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']
if isinstance(review_file, str) and review_file.endswith('.json'):
    with open(review_file, 'r') as f:
        reviews = json.load(f)
else:
    reviews = locals()['var_functions.query_db:30']

# Get businesses from the latest query
businesses = locals()['var_functions.query_db:40']

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({
    "total_reviews": len(reviews),
    "total_businesses": len(businesses),
    "sample_review": reviews[0] if reviews else None
}))

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

var_functions.query_db:10.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!
